verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is canonically a secreted ligand deposited in the ECM as a large latent complex (LLC) via LTBP. However, on activated Tregs, platelets, and myeloid cells, the latent TGFB1 small latent complex (LAP+mature TGF-β1 dimer) is covalently tethered to the outer leaflet via disulfide bonds between LAP Cys33 and GARP (LRRC32), a bona fide transmembrane protein. Similarly, LRRC33 anchors latent TGFB1 on macrophages/microglia. This covalent, wash-resistant anchoring to TM scaffolds constitutes stable surface attachment restricted to specific activated immune cell types. Multiple clinical-stage antibodies targeting the cell-surface GARP:TGF-β1 complex confirm therapeutic accessibility of this surface pool. Outside these cell types, TGFB1 is secreted/ECM-deposited without PM residence.
